content.problemStatement:
We meet our old friend the Despotic King again.  The king was
no believer in democracy (understandably) and did not wish to
share any information regarding the running of his country even
with the nobles in his court. He had a inner circle (a
cabal) consisting of a few old friends from his youth
and couple of proteges and ran the kingdom with their help.  It
was however a benevolent dictatorship and thus there was hardly a
protest either in the court or among the subjects.

The king's sons and potential successors were however of
dubious attitude and quality. Clearly, dictatorship of the
incompetent was the future of the country if the king were to
abdicate or die.  Some of the younger nobles banded together and
confronted the king and demanded openness.

The king promised to appoint a Task Force with members
selected from among the nobles to oversee the administration of
the country.  However he wished to choose its members in such a
way that they would waste all their time fighting each other,
leaving them no opportunity to introduce any changes in the way
the country was being run.

His able advisor, the wily prime minister, gave him a list of
all the pairs of nobles who hated each other.  The king then
wanted the minister to pick as large a Task Force as possible (so
that his generosity was evident), in such a way that each member
of the Task Force hates at least KKK other members (where
KKK was a number to be determined by the king).  The Task
Force cannot be empty.

Note that whenever a pair of nobles "AAA and
BBB" appears in the prime minister's list, it means that
AAA hates BBB and, symmetrically, BBB hates
AAA.

Suppose there are 888 nobles and the prime minister's list says
that the following pairs of nobles "hate each other":


111 and 222
111 and 333
111 and 555
111 and 666
222 and 555
222 and 666
333 and 444
333 and 555
444 and 666
444 and 777
555 and 666
777 and 888
For K=2K = 2K=2, {1,2,3,4,5,6}\{ 1,2,3,4,5,6 \}{1,2,3,4,5,6} is the largest possible
Task Force in which everyone hates at least 222 others. In this
Task Force, 111 hates 222,333,555 and 666, 444 hates 333 and 666, and so on.

On the other hand, if the king sets KKK to 333 then
{1,2,5,6}\{ 1,2,5,6 \}{1,2,5,6} would be largest Task Force in which everyone hates at
least 333 others.  For K=4K = 4K=4 or more, as you can check,
there is no way to group the nobles so that every member hates
KKK or more other members in the group.

Your task is to help the minister determine the size of the
largest Task Force that can be formed from a given set of nobles
in accordance with the king's wishes.

Input:The first line of the input contains three integers
NNN, MMM and KKK.  NNN is
the number of nobles, MMM is the number of pairs of nobles
who hate each other and KKK is the minimum number of members that
each member of the chosen group must hate within the group.

The next MMM lines (lines 2,...,M+12,...,M+12,...,M+1) describe
the "hate" relationship. Line i+1i+1i+1 contains two space
separated integers AiA_iAi​ and
BiB_iBi​, with 1≤Ai<Bi≤N1 \leq A_i < B_i \leq N1≤Ai​<Bi​≤N, indicating that nobles
AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​ hate each
other.

Output:The first line of the output must contain either `YES` or `NO`.

A `YES` in the first line indicates that it is
possible to select a Task Force in which each member hates at
least KKK others. In this case, the second line of the
output contains an integer SSS giving the size of the
largest such Task Force.

A `NO` indicates that it is not possible to form a
Task Force in which each member hates at least KKK others.
In this case the output consists of just one line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains three integers N, M, and K. N is the number of nobles, M is the number of pairs of nobles who hate each other, and K is the minimum number of members that each member of the chosen group must hate within the group. The next M lines describe the 'hate' relationship. Line i+1 contains two space-separated integers Ai and Bi, with 1 ≤ Ai < Bi ≤ N, indicating that nobles Ai and Bi hate each other.
content.outputFormat: The first line of the output must contain either 'YES' or 'NO'. A 'YES' in the first line indicates that it is possible to select a Task Force in which each member hates at least K others. In this case, the second line of the output contains an integer S giving the size of the largest such Task Force. A 'NO' indicates that it is not possible to form a Task Force in which each member hates at least K others.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The largest Task Force in which each member hates at least 2 others is {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}.
The largest Task Force in which each member hates at least 3 others is {1, 2, 5, 6}.
There is no Task Force in which each member hates at least 4 others.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph theory approach. We create a graph where each noble is a node, and two nodes are connected by an edge if the corresponding nobles hate each other. We then need to find the largest clique in this graph such that each node in the clique has at least K neighbors within the clique. This can be done using a backtracking algorithm or a maximal clique algorithm.
solution.timeComplexity: O(2^N) — the time complexity is exponential in the number of nobles, as in the worst case we need to consider all possible subsets of nobles.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the space complexity is linear in the number of nobles, as we need to store the graph and the current clique.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
n, m, k = map(int, input().split())
graph = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
max_clique_size = 0
def backtrack(current_clique, start):
    global max_clique_size
    if start == n: return
    for i in range(start, n):
        valid = True
        for j in range(len(current_clique)):
            if not graph[current_clique[j]][i]:
                valid = False
                break
        if valid:
            current_clique.append(i)
            if len(current_clique) >= max_clique_size and len(current_clique) >= k:
                max_clique_size = len(current_clique)
            backtrack(current_clique, i + 1)
            current_clique.pop()
for _ in range(m):
    a, b = map(int, input().split())
    graph[a - 1][b - 1] = 1
    graph[b - 1][a - 1] = 1
backtrack([], 0)
if max_clique_size >= k:
    print("YES")
    print(max_clique_size)
else:
    print("NO")
